notes.anchor: but he is being instructed to change his name
notes.note: A condition of the bequest required the heir to assume the testatrix's name (a testamentary adoption of sorts); Cicero idly debates whether a young nobleman should change his name to inherit under a woman's will.

notes.anchor: from the eighth hour to evening conferred in private
notes.note: The Roman daylight was divided into twelve hours that varied with the season; the eighth hour falls in mid-afternoon, so the conference with Pompey ran several hours into the evening.

notes.anchor: the speech of Antonius delivered before the assembly
notes.note: M. Antonius (Mark Antony), then a tribune-elect and Caesar's quaestor, harangued a public meeting (contio) on 21 December 50 BC attacking Pompey's whole career; Cicero notes Pompey took even this junior assault as a measure of Caesar's intentions.

notes.anchor: the apparatus of my triumph must be converted to that end
notes.note: Cicero had borrowed money from Caesar and still hoped for a triumph over his Cilician campaign; with war looming he must repay the loan and divert the funds set aside for the triumphal procession, a galling debt to a political opponent.
